title: silene vulgaris subsp. alpina
intro: Silene vulgaris subsp. alpina, also known as alpine catchfly, is a plant species that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Silene vulgaris subsp. alpina include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
